reportingProfile.description: Stakeholder-facing reporting and visualization profile for the historical fixed-cluster LocalAI worker-mode characterization campaigns.

baseline.purpose: Official historical baseline for consolidated LocalAI worker-mode characterization on the fixed two-worker CPU-only Kubernetes cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the initial baseline lightweight and reproducible on the current CPU-only cluster.
Freeze the worker count at two because pilot evidence indicated a clear improvement over a single worker without the added complexity of larger topologies.
Freeze the model at the smallest validated family member (Llama 3.2 1B q4_k_m) to minimize footprint and preserve operational headroom for repeated characterization runs.
Freeze the workload at the controlled low-load scenario WL2 so that subsequent sweeps can vary one dimension at a time from a stable reference point.
Freeze the placement at PL1 because current pilot evidence indicates lower average latency than the distributed two-node alternative in this specific cluster configuration.
lockRules: Do not override model, worker count, placement, or workload from launcher parameters.
Use this baseline as the unique reference configuration until the consolidated characterization phase is complete.
Treat all future deltas as controlled variations from B0.
Do not reuse B0 as the baseline for provider-backed infrastructure profiles; create a new baseline identifier for each new provider-backed cycle.
baseline.notes: This historical baseline intentionally prioritizes stability, reproducibility, and low resource pressure over aggressive scale. The distributed placement scenario PL2 remains available as an experimental comparison, but it is not the locked reference configuration.
baseline.scenarioProfileName: Official historical baseline for consolidated LocalAI worker-mode characterization on the fixed two-worker CPU-only Kubernetes cluster.

payload.description: Completed historical characterization cycle executed on the original fixed two-worker CPU-only K3s cluster before introducing provider-backed infrastructure profiles.

clusterContext.description: Original externally provided K3s cluster used for the first LocalAI worker-mode characterization cycle.
knownMethodologicalLimitations: The cycle is intentionally tied to the original fixed cluster and to node names available in that environment.
Unsupported W3 and W4 worker-count scenarios are retained as valid evidence of current placement/resource constraints rather than measured performance samples.

rules: Do not mutate B0 or the C0 consolidated evidence when starting provider-backed cluster experiments.
Do not overwrite C0 reporting, diagnosis, or completion-gate artifacts to represent future infrastructure-aware runs.
Create a new cycle and a new baseline identifier before running benchmarks on a cluster generated through proxmox-k3s.
Treat C0 as the historical reference cycle for the original fixed cluster, not as the universal baseline for all future infrastructure profiles.
Treat results/experimental-cycles/C0/artifacts as the self-contained frozen evidence snapshot for C0.
A normal freeze rerun must preserve existing C0 artifacts; use an explicit force option only when intentionally rebuilding the historical snapshot.
Treat C0/B0 as bound to infrastructure profile INFRA_ORIGINAL_1CP_2W_8C16G; future provider-backed runs must declare a distinct infrastructure profile.
C0 uses an external retained cluster lifecycle policy and must not trigger provider create or delete operations.
Any lifecycle manifest generated for C0 is descriptive evidence only and does not imply provider-managed provisioning.

nextCyclePreparation.purpose: Provider-backed baseline and infrastructure-aware characterization based on K3s clusters generated through proxmox-k3s.

frozenArtifactPolicy.rationale: The historical cycle must remain self-contained after freezing, while the freeze operation must remain robust when current diagnosis, reporting, or completion-gate artifacts are regenerated with different timestamps before the final lock is created.
frozenArtifactPolicy.sourceResolutionBehavior: technical diagnosis, reporting and completion-gate artifacts are resolved from the canonical C0 cycle-scoped result roots before freeze.
frozenArtifactPolicy.primaryArtifactPolicy: The lock records the latest frozen technical diagnosis JSON, its paired TXT summary, the latest completion-gate JSON/TXT pair, and the stable reporting artifacts.

technicalDiagnosisJson.description: Latest technical diagnosis JSON generated before the C0 freeze is executed.

technicalDiagnosisText.description: Technical diagnosis TXT paired with the resolved primary technical diagnosis JSON.

completionGateJson.description: Latest completion-gate JSON generated before the C0 freeze is executed.

completionGateText.description: Latest completion-gate text summary generated before the C0 freeze is executed.

infrastructureProfile.description: Infrastructure profile formally associated with C0/B0. It describes the original fixed K3s cluster used before introducing provider-backed infrastructure profiles.

cycleGovernance.reason: The original fixed-cluster cycle is preserved as C0 so that C1 can become the first provider-backed baseline cycle generated through proxmox-k3s.

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: The cycle preserves historical fixed-cluster evidence and is not interpreted as a provider-backed materialization.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Single RPC Worker
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. All non-worker-count dimensions are inherited from B0 and the active RPC worker set remains fully co-located on sc-app-02, exactly following the baseline placement mode. The only intended experimental delta is the active worker count.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Single RPC Worker

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Two RPC Workers
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario reproduces the official two-worker baseline layout and keeps both active RPC workers fully co-located on sc-app-02. The only intended experimental delta within the Worker Count Sweep family is the active worker count.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Two RPC Workers

scenario.purpose: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Three RPC Workers
scenario.notes: Worker-count-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. Model, workload, prompt, server manifest, and placement mode are all inherited from B0; the third active RPC worker is added while preserving the same co-located placement on sc-app-02 so that only the active worker count varies.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Worker Count Sweep - Three RPC Workers

unsupportedReports.reason: Worker-count scenario unsupported under the current constraints of the fixed baseline: placement 'colocated_sc_app_02' must remain unchanged and, after auto-apply and the configured stabilization window, at least one RPC pod required by the scenario remains unschedulable in the current cluster due to resource and/or affinity constraints.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Smoke Single User
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the single-user smoke workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Workload Sweep - Smoke Single User

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Low Load Two Users
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the low-load two-user reference workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Workload Sweep - Low Load Two Users

scenario.purpose: Pilot Workload Sweep - Small Concurrency Four Users
scenario.notes: Workload-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario defines the small-concurrency four-user workload while inheriting the fixed model, worker scenario, placement scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Workload Sweep family is the resolved input workload profile.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Workload Sweep - Small Concurrency Four Users

scenario.purpose: Pilot Model Sweep - Baseline Llama 3.2 1B q4_k_m
scenario.notes: Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario reproduces the official baseline model and inherits the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Model Sweep - Baseline Llama 3.2 1B q4_k_m

scenario.notes: Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 1B q8_0 variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 3B q4_k_m variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.

scenario.purpose: Pilot Model Sweep - Llama 3.2 3B Instruct q8_0
scenario.notes: Model-only variation anchored to the official locked baseline B0. This scenario changes the model family member to the 3B q8_0 variant while inheriting the fixed worker scenario, placement scenario, workload scenario, prompt, temperature, and request timeout directly from the baseline. The only intended experimental delta within the Model Sweep family is the resolved model and its derived server manifest.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Pilot Model Sweep - Llama 3.2 3B Instruct q8_0

scenario.purpose: Historical compatibility placement scenario for co-located LocalAI server and RPC workers.
scenario.notes: Placement-only variation anchored to the historical baseline. This scenario now resolves to the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile while preserving the original co-located sc-app-02 topology and keeping model, worker count, workload, prompt, temperature, and request timeout fixed.
scenario.placementResearchQuestion: How does a compact LocalAI topology behave when communication distance is minimized but CPU and memory contention may increase?
scenario.canonicalNamingPolicy: Use placementProfileId for provider-backed cycles; keep PL1/PL2 only for historical fixed-cluster and legacy pilot compatibility.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Historical compatibility placement scenario for co-located LocalAI server and RPC workers.

scenario.purpose: Historical compatibility placement scenario for distributed two-node LocalAI RPC worker placement.
scenario.notes: Placement-only variation anchored to the historical baseline. This scenario now resolves to the explicit PL_DISTRIBUTED_TWO_NODE placement profile while preserving the original distributed two-node worker topology and keeping model, worker count, workload, prompt, temperature, and request timeout fixed.
scenario.placementResearchQuestion: Does spreading RPC workers across two nodes reduce local resource contention enough to compensate for the additional inter-node communication path?
scenario.canonicalNamingPolicy: Use placementProfileId for provider-backed cycles; keep PL1/PL2 only for historical fixed-cluster and legacy pilot compatibility.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Historical compatibility placement scenario for distributed two-node LocalAI RPC worker placement.
